name: Harriet A. Washington
practice: Medical ethics, history of medical racism, environmental racism in health
approach: Medical abuse of Black Americans isn't historical — it's structural and ongoing. From Tuskegee to toxic waste siting to algorithmic bias in care allocation. The health system itself is a social determinant of health for Black people.
body_of_work: Medical Apartheid (2006) — comprehensive history of medical experimentation on Black Americans. A Terrible Thing to Waste (2019) — environmental racism's neurological damage. Carte Blanche (2021) — vaccine hesitancy's rational roots in medical abuse.
why_instructive: The dome must reckon with the fact that its own systems — health, legal, housing — have been instruments of harm. Her work ensures the dome sees its own shadow.

dome_relevance.1: Legal history of medical experimentation without consent
dome_relevance.4: Health — medical racism as ongoing structural force
dome_relevance.8: Community — trust destruction and its consequences

sphere_relevance.3: Environmental racism — toxic facilities sited near Black communities
sphere_relevance.5: Social — racial demographics predict environmental exposure
sphere_relevance.9: Policy — how zoning and siting decisions encode racism
